blocks.title: Plumbing Invoice Template
blocks.subtitle: A professional invoice template designed specifically for plumbers. Easily bill for emergency call-out fees, parts, and labor.

blocks.authorBio: Jane is a certified CPA with over 15 years of experience in small business accounting.

blocks.heading: Why Plumbers Need specialized Invoices
blocks.text: Plumbing jobs range from quick 30-minute leak repairs to massive week-long commercial installations. Your invoice needs the flexibility to handle flat-rate emergency call-out fees, hourly labor rates, and detailed parts lists. Using a specialized plumbing invoice ensures your clients understand exactly what they are paying for, reducing friction and helping you collect payment on the spot.

summaryItems: Always list your emergency call-out fee or diagnostic fee separately.
Itemize specific parts used (e.g., '3/4 inch PVC Valve') rather than grouping them.
Include your master plumbing license number to build trust.
State your warranty or guarantee terms clearly in the notes section.

steps.title: Add Your Business and License Info
steps.description: Add your plumbing business name, contact info, and state/local master plumber license numbers.

steps.title: Log the Service Address
steps.description: Plumbers often work for landlords or property managers. Ensure the 'Billing Address' (the landlord) and the 'Service Address' (where the work was done) are both noted.

steps.title: Itemize Labor and Parts
steps.description: List labor hours and rates on one line, and specific parts (copper piping, fixtures, sealants) on subsequent lines.

steps.title: Include Warranty Information
steps.description: Use the notes section at the bottom to state something like, '30-Day Guarantee on all labor and installed parts.'

mistakes.solution: If you arrive at a job and there is existing water damage, note it on the invoice or estimate before starting work to avoid liability disputes later.
Include a link or QR code on your digital invoice that allows the customer to pay via credit card immediately before you leave the driveway.

questions.question: Can I charge a flat diagnostic fee?
questions.description: Yes. It is standard industry practice to charge a flat 'Call-out' or 'Diagnostic' fee just to assess the problem, which can then be credited toward the final repair bill if they hire you.

blocks.heading: Ready to get paid?
blocks.subtext: Use our free invoice generator to create a professional plumbing invoice before you leave the job site.
